subtopic: Amino acid general formula, acid-base properties, and dipolar ion (zwitterion) form
Classification of amino acids (nonpolar, polar, charged, aromatic, special cases)
Absolute configuration at the α carbon (L vs D; stereochemistry conventions)
Reactions of amino acids with acids and bases; titration and pI
Peptide bond formation by condensation and hydrolysis of peptide bonds
Sulfur linkage for cysteine and cystine; disulfide bonds
Peptide linkage polarity; resonance and partial double-bond character of peptide bond
Primary structure (amino acid sequence); secondary structure (α-helix, β-sheet, turns)
Tertiary and quaternary structure; forces stabilizing each level (hydrophobic, ionic, H-bond, disulfide)
Common structural motifs in proteins and peptides (coiled-coil, β-barrel, zinc finger, leucine zipper)
Simple, conjugated, and derived proteins; fibrous vs globular proteins
Denaturation of proteins: agents and consequences
Non-enzymatic protein function (structural, transport, signaling, motor, immune)
Myosin and actin as structural and motor protein examples
Nucleotide composition, structure, and function (purines vs pyrimidines; phosphate, sugar, base)
DNA structure (double helix, antiparallel strands, base pairing, B-form) and RNA structure/types (mRNA, tRNA, rRNA)
Other nucleotide functions: ATP as energy currency; NAD+, FAD as electron carriers; cAMP as second messenger
Transcription: mechanism, RNA polymerase, promoters, termination; mRNA processing (capping, polyadenylation, splicing)
Translation: ribosomes, tRNA charging, initiation/elongation/termination; genetic code (codons, wobble, degeneracy)
DNA replication: semi-conservative model, origins, helicase, primase, DNA polymerase, Okazaki fragments, proofreading
DNA repair mechanisms (base excision, nucleotide excision, mismatch repair, double-strand break repair)
PCR, restriction enzymes, gel electrophoresis, Southern/Northern blotting, hybridization probes
Vectors, transfection, RFLP, DNA sequencing (Sanger, next-gen concepts)
Gene expression differences: operons (prokaryote); introns/exons, splicing, nuclear envelope (eukaryote); coupled transcription-translation
Post-translational modifications (phosphorylation, glycosylation, ubiquitination, cleavage, signal peptides)
Chromatin structure, histone proteins, nucleosome, solenoid (30-nm fiber), supercoiling
Single-copy vs repetitive DNA; heterochromatin vs euchromatin; sister chromatids, centromeres, telomeres
Mendelian concepts: phenotype, genotype, gene, allele, dominant/recessive; Punnett square
Meiosis I and II stages; comparison with mitosis; ploidy; segregation; independent assortment
Crossing over and recombination; homologous chromosomes vs sister chromatids
Codominance, incomplete dominance, multiple alleles, pleiotropy, epistasis
Recombination frequencies and chromosome mapping; linkage
Sex-linked characteristics; phenotypic sex determination (SRY gene, sex chromosomes); cytoplasmic/extranuclear inheritance (mitochondrial)
Types of mutations: point, silent, missense, nonsense, frameshift; inversion, deletion, translocation
Environmental mutagens; base-pair substitutions; random nature of mutations
Regulatory proteins (transcription factors, repressors, activators); prokaryotic operons (lac operon, trp operon)
Eukaryotic gene regulation: chromatin remodeling, DNA methylation, histone acetylation/methylation, X-inactivation
Oncogenes, proto-oncogenes, and tumor suppressor genes in gene regulation
Natural selection: fitness, differential reproduction, rate of allele frequency change
Hardy-Weinberg Principle: conditions, equations (p² + 2pq + q² = 1), use in population genetics
Adaptation: inbreeding effects, heterozygote advantage (sickle cell example)
Speciation: prezygotic vs postzygotic isolation; hybridization
Genetic drift: founder effect and bottleneck effect
Free energy: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS; spontaneous vs nonspontaneous reactions; exergonic vs endergonic
Coupled reactions; activation energy; enzymes and catalysis overview
Phosphorylation-dephosphorylation; high-energy phosphate bonds; ATP hydrolysis ΔG; ATP as phosphate-group donor
Half-reactions; standard reduction potential (E°); energetics of electron transfer; NADH/FADH₂ as carriers; energy released transferring from NADH to O₂
Glycolysis: substrates, products (pyruvate, ATP, NADH), energetics, key enzymes, regulation; anaerobic fermentation (lactate, ethanol)
Krebs cycle (TCA): substrates (acetyl-CoA), products (CO₂, NADH, FADH₂, GTP), regulation, key enzymes
Mitochondrial electron transport chain (complexes I–IV); proton-motive force; ATP synthase; net ATP yield per glucose; uncouplers and coupling agents
Gluconeogenesis: substrates (lactate, glycerol, amino acids), bypass enzymes, regulation vs glycolysis
Glycogenolysis and glycogenesis: substrates, products, key enzymes (glycogen phosphorylase, glycogen synthase), regulation
Pentose phosphate pathway: main products (NADPH, ribose-5-phosphate) and function (biosynthesis, antioxidant)
Hormonal regulation of carbohydrate metabolism: insulin, glucagon, epinephrine pathways
Lipid classification: triacylglycerols, phospholipids, sphingolipids, steroids, terpenes, waxes, prostaglandins, fat-soluble vitamins; structural vs functional lipids
Digestion, absorption, and transport of dietary fats (bile, micelles, chylomicrons, lipoproteins)
β-oxidation of fatty acids: products (acetyl-CoA, NADH, FADH₂), energetics, location (mitochondrial matrix)
Ketone body synthesis (ketogenesis) in liver; use by brain and heart during fasting/starvation
Fatty acid synthesis: location (cytoplasm), acetyl-CoA carboxylase, malonyl-CoA, NADPH requirement; contrast with β-oxidation
Amino acid catabolism: transamination, oxidative deamination; urea cycle (substrates, products, location)
Amino acids as biosynthetic precursors (neurotransmitters, heme, nucleotides); essential vs non-essential amino acids
Fluid mosaic model; lipid bilayer composition; integral and peripheral membrane proteins
Membrane receptors; glycoproteins and glycolipids; membrane asymmetry
Simple diffusion; osmosis; osmotic pressure and tonicity (hypo-, iso-, hypertonic solutions)
Facilitated diffusion (channel proteins, carrier proteins); active transport; cotransport; Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase pump
Endocytosis and exocytosis (phagocytosis, pinocytosis, receptor-mediated endocytosis)
Mitochondria: inner/outer membrane, cristae, matrix; function in ATP synthesis; semi-autonomous (own DNA, self-replication)
Lysosomes: hydrolytic enzymes, compartmentalization, pH; lysosomal storage diseases concept
Endoplasmic reticulum: rough (protein synthesis, glycosylation) vs smooth (lipid synthesis, detoxification); secretory pathway
Golgi apparatus: cis/trans faces, vesicle trafficking, protein sorting and modification, secretory function
Nucleus: nuclear envelope, nuclear pores, nucleolus; genetic material organization
Cytoskeleton components: actin (microfilaments), microtubules, intermediate filaments; motor proteins (myosin, kinesin, dynein); cell motility and shape
Prokaryotic cell structure: cell wall (peptidoglycan), pili, flagella, capsule, ribosome type; binary fission; plasmids
Bacterial growth phases; endospore formation; gram-positive vs gram-negative distinction
Fungal characteristics: heterotrophic, chitin cell wall; unicellular (yeast) vs multicellular (mold); sexual and asexual reproduction
Virus structure (capsid, envelope, genetic material — DNA vs RNA, ss vs ds); lytic vs lysogenic cycles; bacteriophage vs animal virus
Innate vs adaptive immunity; first-line and second-line defenses; inflammation; complement system
Antibody structure (heavy/light chains, variable/constant regions, Fab/Fc); antibody classes (IgG, IgM, IgA, IgE, IgD); diversity generation (VDJ recombination)
B and T cell maturation; clonal selection and expansion; memory cells
Antigen presentation: MHC class I (all nucleated cells, CD8 cytotoxic T) vs MHC class II (APCs, CD4 helper T); cell-mediated vs humoral immunity
Gas exchange at alveoli; lung volumes and capacities (tidal, vital capacity, residual); pulmonary circulation; ventilation-perfusion matching
Control of breathing: medullary rhythmicity, chemoreceptors (CO₂/pH, O₂); Bohr effect and oxygen-hemoglobin dissociation curve
Four-chambered heart anatomy; cardiac cycle (systole/diastole); conduction system (SA node, AV node, bundle of His, Purkinje fibers)
Arteries, veins, capillaries: structure and function; blood pressure regulation; Starling forces at capillary beds
ABO and Rh blood type genetics and transfusion compatibility; blood clotting (coagulation cascade, platelets, fibrin)
Lymphatic system: lymph nodes, lymphatic vessels, fluid balance; role in immune surveillance and fat absorption (lacteals)
Gross anatomy of GI tract; digestion and absorption of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins; intestinal villi/microvilli
Gastric secretions (HCl, pepsin, mucus); liver (bile production, detox, glycogen storage); gallbladder (bile concentration); pancreas (digestive enzymes, bicarbonate)
GI hormones: gastrin, secretin, CCK, GIP; large intestine function (water reabsorption, microbiome)
Kidney anatomy; nephron structure (glomerulus, proximal tubule, loop of Henle, distal tubule, collecting duct); filtration, reabsorption, secretion, excretion
Urine composition; hormonal regulation: ADH (water reabsorption), aldosterone (Na⁺/K⁺), ANP (natriuresis); renin-angiotensin system
Skeletal vs smooth vs cardiac muscle: structural and functional differences; sarcomere organization (actin, myosin, titin, Z-lines)
Sliding filament mechanism; troponin-tropomyosin complex; calcium role in contraction; cross-bridge cycle
Neuromuscular junction: ACh release, end-plate potential; motor unit; slow-twitch (type I) vs fast-twitch (type II) fibers
Bone types (long, short, flat, irregular); bone composition (organic matrix: collagen; inorganic: hydroxyapatite); bone cells (osteoblast, osteoclast, osteocyte); endochondral ossification
Joint types (fibrous, cartilaginous, synovial); joint function and range of motion
Skin layers: epidermis (stratum corneum, basale, spinosum, granulosum) and dermis; thermoregulation; sensory receptors; wound healing
Male reproductive system: testes, spermatogenesis, testosterone regulation (HPG axis); structures (epididymis, vas deferens, prostate)
Female reproductive system: ovary, oogenesis, folliculogenesis; menstrual cycle (follicular, ovulatory, luteal phases); FSH, LH, estrogen, progesterone
Fertilization; implantation; placenta (gas exchange, nutrient transfer, hormone production: hCG, progesterone)
Cell cycle phases: G₀, G₁, S, G₂, M; checkpoints (G₁/S, G₂/M, spindle assembly); cyclins and CDKs as regulators
Mitosis stages: prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase; cytokinesis; cleavage furrow vs cell plate
Meiosis I and II stages; crossing over (prophase I); nondisjunction and aneuploidy; ploidy changes
Fertilization → cleavage → morula → blastula; gastrulation; three germ layers (ectoderm, mesoderm, endoderm) and their derivatives
Neurulation: neural plate, neural tube, neural crest cells and their fates; developmental principles (induction, competence, determination)
Oncogenes (activating mutations, proto-oncogene conversion) and tumor suppressor genes (Rb, p53) as cell cycle regulators; cancer as loss of proliferation control
Stem cell potency levels (totipotent, pluripotent, multipotent, unipotent); embryonic vs adult stem cells; therapeutic and research applications
CNS vs PNS divisions; somatic vs autonomic nervous system; sympathetic vs parasympathetic: neurotransmitters, effects, fight-or-flight vs rest-and-digest

topic: Nervous System — Neuron Structure and Physiology
subtopic: Neuron anatomy (cell body, dendrites, axon, myelin sheath); resting membrane potential: Na⁺/K⁺ concentrations, selective permeability, membrane potential (~−70 mV)

topic: Nervous System — Neuron Structure and Physiology
subtopic: Action potential: depolarization (Na⁺ influx), repolarization (K⁺ efflux), refractory periods (absolute/relative); all-or-none principle; saltatory conduction
Synapse types (chemical vs electrical); neurotransmitter release, binding, and receptor types (ionotropic vs metabotropic); EPSP and IPSP; spatial and temporal summation
Major neurotransmitters: acetylcholine, dopamine, norepinephrine, serotonin, GABA, glutamate, glycine; their general functions and target effects
Glial cell types and functions: astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, Schwann cells, microglia, ependymal cells; blood-brain barrier
Visual system: photoreceptors (rods vs cones), retina organization, optic nerve; visual transduction (rhodopsin, cGMP, hyperpolarization)
Auditory system: cochlea, basilar membrane, hair cells; tonotopic organization; vestibular system (semicircular canals, otolith organs)
Olfactory and gustatory reception; olfactory receptor neurons; taste receptor types (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, umami); somatosensory pain pathways (nociceptors, dorsal horn, spinothalamic tract)
Hormone classification: amino acid-derived (epinephrine, thyroid hormones), peptide (insulin, GH, ADH), steroid (cortisol, sex hormones); mechanisms of action
Hypothalamus–anterior pituitary–target gland axis; releasing and inhibiting hormones; negative feedback loops; posterior pituitary (ADH, oxytocin direct release)
Thyroid (T3/T4, calcitonin), parathyroid (PTH); calcium homeostasis; thyroid disorders concept (hypo/hyperthyroidism)
Adrenal cortex (cortisol, aldosterone, androgens) and adrenal medulla (epinephrine, norepinephrine); stress response
Pancreas: insulin (β-cells, glucose uptake) and glucagon (α-cells, glycogenolysis/gluconeogenesis); diabetes concept; pineal gland (melatonin); gonads (estrogen, testosterone)
cAMP/PKA pathway (β-adrenergic, glucagon receptors); IP3/DAG/PKC pathway; receptor tyrosine kinase (insulin, growth factors); nuclear receptors (steroid hormones)
Reflex arcs: monosynaptic (stretch reflex) and polysynaptic (withdrawal reflex); afferent/efferent pathways; role of spinal cord
Pain (nociception): nociceptors, pain fiber types (Aδ, C fibers), gate control theory, endogenous opioids (endorphins, enkephalins)
Circadian rhythms: suprachiasmatic nucleus as biological clock; melatonin production and light regulation; jet lag and shift work disruption
Ethological concepts: fixed action patterns, sign stimuli, imprinting (critical periods), habituation, sensitization; innate vs learned behavior
Animal communication signals (visual, auditory, chemical/pheromones, tactile); honest vs deceptive signaling
Reproductive behavior: pair bonding, parental care strategies; mate selection and sexual selection (intrasexual competition, intersexual choice)
Sociobiology: altruism, inclusive fitness, kin selection (Hamilton's rule), reciprocal altruism; group living benefits and costs
